mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal sustained labor demand and consumption capacity, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:January 2023 nonfarm payrolls surprised significantly to the upside
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases, or the Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 4.25% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve raised rates to multi-year highs with effective rate near 4.33% in January 2023
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.25%, or the negative mean return during triggered periods is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and a high federal funds rate jointly imply restrictive real policy rates that erode demand and compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Headline inflation remained above 6% year-over-year in early 2023 while Fed funds exceeded 4%
failure_condition: A predictive regression shows the CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS interaction term carries a positive or statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.05) for 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the interaction adds less than 2 percentage points of R-squared over main effects.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions with tight labor markets, sequential declines in housing starts (HOUST) foreshadow broader demand deterioration and falling corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts weakened through late 2022 while unemployment stayed near 50-year lows
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following weak HOUST readings are not significantly lower when UNRATE is below 4% compared to when UNRATE is at or above 4% (t-test p > 0.10), or the signal Rank IC is above zero.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing industrial production (INDPRO) and a federal funds rate above 4% captures a profits-squeeze dynamic where restrictive policy meets weakening supply, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production growth stalled in late 2022 as the policy rate exceeded 4%
regime_hint: restrictive policy coinciding with supply slowdown
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and FEDFUNDS in a regression for 20-day S&P 500 returns yields a positive or insignificant coefficient (p > 0.05), or the interaction-driven signal underperforms a simple INDPRO-only model.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving manufacturing throughput and corporate margin expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive changes in INDPRO fail to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or the Rank IC between INDPRO changes and forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points over a three-month window triggers recession-risk repricing and broad equity de-risking, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Forward-looking labor market indicators showed deterioration despite a 3.4% unemployment rate in January 2023
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of instances where the UNRATE trigger fires, or the signal hit rate is below 45% over any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 6%, elevated inflation surprises prompt expectations of further Fed hawkishness and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:January 2023 CPI data showed headline inflation remained above 6% year-over-year
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 6%, or the negative mean return during triggered periods is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).
